opp.ais_why:
• Per the 19 Jan 2026 rep note Astrid set expectations: 'Good briefing from Astrid (rfi coming) and phase 1 focus is P2P. Looking at Coupa 2 others this week.' The buyer explicitly framed Phase 1 as P2P-focused, not AI-focused. On 10 Feb 2026: 'RFI out (ck meeting with exec and working team today (10th) to influence- believed to have 3 in race (Coupa pushing AI?)' ‚Äî AI surfaces as a competitive dynamic the rep is monitoring, not as a Cebu Pacific stated requirement.
• On 13 Mar 2026: 'long demo session was ok- with a refocus from CP on finance.' On 11 May 2026: 'Confirmed meeting CPO F2F 20th.' On 4 May 2026: 'Request to meet Rohan (CPO) while process has slowed temporarily- f2f as well as Horizon SEA.' Champion is CPO Rohan; agentic AI is in product scope (the only AI SKU listed); but the captured buyer conversation centres on finance refocus and P2P phase 1, not AI capability.
• Confidence is Medium. Agentic AI is in product scope and there is competitive pressure ('Coupa pushing AI'), but the buyer's own framing has been P2P-first with a finance refocus. Held at 5 (boundary DOWN per rubric) ‚Äî the RFI itself would need to mandate AI to move this to AI Hungry; right now it's reactive to competitor positioning.

items.quote: we put a lot of constraints right now from a cash flow perspective, from a Capex perspective

items.source: Next Step 13 Mar 2026; Mark Cezar absent from all 2026 calls

items.area: Paper process / InfoSec / SAP integration scope
items.source: No MSA/SOW/InfoSec discussion in any 2026 source

sources: Next Step 13 Mar 2026 'refocus from CP on finance'
metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated clear operational pain but has not attached a single number to it. Astrid Tanquieng described the environment on 19 Jan 2026 as 'our process is highly manual' with 'fragmented systems' and non-PO payment requests that cannot be monitored. On 19 May 2026 the finance-side speaker framed the whole initiative through cash-flow and Capex constraints rather than a savings target. No cost-per-invoice, exception rate, cycle-time, or headcount figure has been quantified on any call, and no ROI or value workshop has run. Building a quantified Phase 1 business case (touchless invoice rate, AP FTE hours, exception reduction) is the move that unlocks finance sign-off.

sources: Next Step ~6 Jun 2026 'Zycus clear favored vendor'
champion.narrative: Championship is developing but not yet powerful. Astrid Tanquieng is the engaged working-level owner who briefed Zycus, coordinates the process, and shared the buyer's P2P pain points, but she is a gatekeeper and told the SM Retail Zycus story warmly without pushing back on rivals or opening the CFO. What has developed is senior sponsorship: Rohan Kapoor (Group Director - Operations Procurement) called Zycus the 'clear favored vendor', is actively pursuing the relationship (requesting ProcureCon passes, F2F meetings), and is bringing his team plus Anna Pamela Dela Pasion to both ProcureCon and Horizon SEA. What is still missing is an advocate who will carry a Phase 1 business case to CFO Mark Cezar and drive urgency against the funding pause. The two July events are the moment to convert Rohan from favorable sponsor into active champion.

competition.narrative: Zycus holds the favored position but has not closed the field out. Coupa is the live named rival and remains in the mix as of ~6 Jun 2026 ('Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix'), positioning AI as its differentiator ('3 in race, Coupa pushing AI?', 10 Feb 2026). SAP Ariba sits in the Salesforce competitor field but has not surfaced in any 2026 call, so it is dormant. The strongest actual threat is not a vendor but the do-nothing/delay path driven by funding caution and the SAP S/4 sequencing. Zycus wins by formalizing the Agentic AI / Merlin wedge for AP and intake and by offering the phased, low-year-one commercial structure Carl floated on 19 May, giving finance a way to progress within budget before Coupa re-engages.

sources: Avoma discovery call 19 Jan 2026 (Astrid Tanquieng, Akshay)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Hazel B Navarro, Agnes Alvarez
identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well established and owner-attributed. Astrid Tanquieng (Manager, Operations Systems and Services Contracts) detailed on 19 Jan 2026 a highly manual, fragmented AP process with no way to monitor payment requests, a need to automate non-PO payment requests and auto-process low-value/low-risk invoices, and PO matching problems. Akshay (Zycus) reframed the root cause as the upstream SAP PR/PO quality driving downstream invoice exceptions, which the buyer accepted. The pain owners are the AP and finance organizations (Hazel Navarro, Operations Accounting Manager; Agnes Alvarez, Assistant Manager AP) under CFO Mark Cezar, with procurement (Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion) sponsoring. The pain is real and acknowledged but not urgent enough to override the funding pause.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Anne Tieng (VP Legal), Katrina Reynoso (IT compliance)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are barely started. The only paper activity is the MNDA released with the RFI on 30 Jan 2026 and a request for a revised NDA with complete details on 21 Mar 2026, both handled by Luk/Astrid. No MSA, SOW, order form, InfoSec review, or SAP S/4 integration scoping has been discussed on any call, and no procurement/legal timeline exists because the RFP is paused. Anne Tieng (VP Legal Affairs) and Katrina Reynoso (IT/compliance) are mapped as the eventual legal and InfoSec gates. Visibility will come only once the phased proposal is accepted and a mutual close plan is drafted.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Mark Cezar, CFO, named as the primary Salesforce contact, and the initiative has visibly moved under finance control ('refocus from CP on finance', Next Step 13 Mar 2026). Finance ownership was reinforced on 19 May 2026 when the buyer said 'we are finance and we report this' and framed the decision around cash flow and Capex. The gap is engagement: Mark Cezar has not appeared on any 2026 call or email thread, and it was finance caution that paused the RFP. Rohan Kapoor (Group Director, senior procurement sponsor) and Anna Pamela Dela Pasion (Director Procurement) carry the process, but the budget release sits with the CFO, who must be brought into a value conversation.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured RFI-then-RFP process: the RFI Letter of Invitation plus MNDA released 30 Jan 2026, a vendor demo 5 Mar 2026, and an RFP planned to follow. Astrid described talking to 'several providers' with roughly three vendors believed to be in the race (Next Step 10 Feb 2026). That process is currently paused ('The RFP has been paused', 8 May 2026) pending funding clarity, and the buyer is exploring a smaller phased entry. The approval chain runs through procurement (Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion) up to the CFO for budget, with the SAP S/4 HANA cutover (Q3/Q4 next year) as a sequencing dependency the buyer raised on 19 May 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are emerging rather than locked. On 19 Jan 2026 Astrid Tanquieng named the priorities: automating the manual AP and P2P downstream (non-PO payment requests, low-value/low-risk invoice auto-processing, PO matching), integration with their existing SAP ('whatever system we choose for p two p should be able to integrate with that'), and a user-friendly system for change management ('as part of change management... it would help to have a very user friendly system'). She also asked specifically to see airline clients and use cases. She stated plainly 'we haven't firmed up the actual criteria by which we will evaluate the proposals', so criteria are confirmed in theme but not yet formalized in a scored RFP.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Thin signals pull the read to 34.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Astrid Tanquieng is a developing working-level champion (engaged, coordinates the process, warm on the SM Retail Zycus history) but has not opened the CFO or defended Zycus against Coupa; senior sponsor Rohan Kapoor calls Zycus the favored vendor and is bringing his team to both July events, which is the moment to convert him into an active advocate. Missing: an advocate who carries a Phase 1 business case to CFO Mark Cezar.

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is the buyer-stated favored vendor as of ~6 Jun 2026, so no rival is currently ahead.

Draft a phased, low-year-one commercial proposal for Cebu Pacific finance. (by 20 Jul)
Build a quantified Phase 1 AP business case (touchless rate, exception and FTE savings) to bring CFO Mark Cezar into a value conversation. (by 18 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Cebu Pacific Air — Formal Evaluation · $300K · closes 30 Jul

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 34 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 19 is anchored on the stage win (34), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5.0 / Moderate. The buyer is interested in agentic AI but explicitly wants to fix manual AP first ('we'll focus on fixing the manual processing first before we even go into that', 19 Jan 2026), so appetite is real but sequenced behind core automation. Calls confirm the field read rather than under- or over-rating it.

RFP paused pending funding clarity and systems going live in coming months

Ownership reassigned to Carl Kimball (VP Asia Pacific)

deal_movement.summary: The deal is live and relationship-warm (active two-way email through June, buyer team confirmed for two July events) but the formal evaluation is paused for funding, so commercial momentum has thinned even as engagement stays high.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warm (active June email threads, buyer team confirmed for two July events), but the commercial track is stalled on funding, so the deal is progressing relationally while the decision has slowed.

north_star_verdict.math: 27 days to close (close 2026-07-30, swept 2026-07-03). Stage Formal Evaluation entered 19 Jan 2026 (qualified date), roughly 5.5 months in stage; RFI released 30 Jan 2026, RFP planned but paused 8 May 2026. No pricing, commercial proposal, contract, or InfoSec work has started in any 2026 call or activity. Buyer stated the project will slow due to funding caution (Next Step ~6 Jun 2026) and the SAP S/4 HANA cutover is Q3/Q4 next year, both sequencing a full decision well past 30 Jul. Close date last pushed 30 Jun to 30 Jul on 11 May 2026 without a buyer-confirmed decision date; a further multi-month slip is required.
evidence: Astrid Tanquieng emailed 8 May 2026: 'The RFP has been paused for...' confirming the evaluation is on hold (Task 08 May 2026)

Next Step ~6 Jun 2026: 'Project will slow due to funding caution. Is looking for a smaller way to progress- ie start with sourcing/Intake and spend- currently reviewing a new proposal to submit- Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix'
Buyer (finance speaker) 19 May 2026: 'we put a lot of constraints right now from a cash flow perspective, from a Capex perspective' (Avoma follow-up 19 May 2026)
No pricing, commercial proposal, or contract mechanics in any 2026 call, Next Step, or activity

Buyer team (Rohan Kapoor, Anna Pamela Dela Pasion, Astrid Tanquieng) confirmed for ProcureCon Singapore 8-9 Jul and Horizon SEA 21 Jul (Tasks 02-18 Jun 2026) shows the relationship is live despite the pause
north_star_verdict.headline: Cebu Pacific is engaged and has Zycus as the clear favored vendor, but the buyer explicitly paused the RFP for funding caution and is de-scoping to a smaller Phase 1, so the 30 Jul close cannot hold and momentum on the commercial track has thinned.

flags: Turn the ProcureCon 8-9 Jul and Horizon 21 Jul F2Fs into a working session on the phased Phase 1 scope, not just relationship-building, while Rohan and Anna are in the room.
CFO Mark Cezar owns the budget and finance drove the RFP pause, yet he has never joined a call; a phased ROI put in front of finance is the gating lever.
Coupa is still in the mix positioning AI; formalize the Agentic AI / Merlin wedge for AP and intake to lock the favored position before the RFP resumes.
best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is favored but the deal is paused on funding; the win levers are converting the July face-to-faces into a working Phase 1 session, pulling the CFO into a value conversation, and formalizing the AI wedge against Coupa.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the buyer-stated favored vendor as of ~6 Jun 2026, so no rival is currently ahead. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing/delay path from funding caution and the SAP S/4 HANA cutover (Q3/Q4 next year), which paused the RFP. Coupa is the live named rival, still in the mix and pushing AI, but the buyer leans Zycus. SAP Ariba is in the Salesforce field but dormant with no 2026 mention.

competitors.quote: Project will slow due to funding caution. Is looking for a smaller way to progress

competitors.how_we_win: Offer a small, low-year-one Phase 1 (AP + intake/spend) that delivers value inside the funding constraint and paves the way for the full S2P at SAP S/4 cutover

competitors.quote: Zycus clear favored vendor, though Coupa still in the mix

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.source: SF Competitors field (no 2026 call or Next Step mention)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as ERP-agnostic AP/P2P layer over SAP ECC/S4 with official adapters, avoiding a full SAP-native lock-in

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI is the only AI SKU in scope and Coupa is pushing AI, but the buyer frames Phase 1 as P2P/finance-first and the Merlin narrative has not been put in front of them in a proposal, so Zycus is under-positioned on its strongest wedge.

items.quote: we put a lot of constraints right now from a cash flow perspective, from a Capex perspective

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus aligns well on the core AP/P2P automation and SAP-integration asks and offers the phased flexibility the buyer needs; the open exposures are a quantified value case and airline-specific proof.
